name: no-public-claude-tokenizer-use-cl100k-approx
tags: tokenizer token count tiktoken claude anthropic cl100k_base approximation context size budget task:3369b86a
body:
Anthropic does not publish a Claude tokenizer, so exact token counts for Claude prompts aren't computable locally. tiktoken's cl100k_base encoding (GPT-3.5/4 family) is the closest practical stand-in.

Why: needed real token counts (not chars/4) for UPS context-size logging in claude-hooks; no Claude-specific option exists.

How to apply: for any future token-budget/context-size work against Claude prompts in this repo (or elsewhere), reach for src/tools/tokens.py's count_tokens() (cl100k_base) rather than re-deriving a heuristic — treat the result as a close approximation, not exact, especially for code-heavy or non-English text.
